proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_057 (an ordinal 0/1/2 covariate) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_057 is 0.655 in responders vs 0.826 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=2.61e-103). Response rate falls monotonically across feature_057 levels: 0 -> 0.213, 1 -> 0.155, 2 -> 0.112.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_011 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of the continuous variable feature_006 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 = 3.383 in responders vs 3.952 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=7.20e-39); higher values predict lower response.
Mean feature_006 = 58.233 in responders vs 60.309 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=9.50e-20).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the right-skewed continuous variable feature_063 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Higher values of the right-skewed continuous variable feature_092 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on raw and log1p-transformed feature_063
analyses.result_summary: feature_063 means: responders 5.654, non-responders 6.166 (t-test p=4.37e-07). After log1p: 1.527 vs 1.574 (p=6.57e-07).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind on raw and log1p-transformed feature_092
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 means: responders 19.074, non-responders 20.401 (t-test p=1.30e-05). After log1p: 2.570 vs 2.633 (p=3.37e-09).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_099 are associated with a higher probability of objective_response (opposite direction from feature_011/006/063/092).

analyses.result_summary: feature_099 = 3.825 in responders vs 3.796 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=1.30e-06); higher values modestly predict higher response, opposite to most other continuous predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.221 when feature_035=1 vs 0.165 when 0 (chi-square p=3.52e-18); absolute difference +0.056.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_093=1 is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than feature_093=0.
feature_121=1 is associated with a lower probability of objective_response than feature_121=0.
feature_014=1 is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than feature_014=0.
feature_030=1 is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than feature_030=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_093=1 response rate 0.185 vs feature_093=0 rate 0.168 (chi-square p=0.00564).
feature_121=1 response rate 0.156 vs feature_121=0 rate 0.170 (chi-square p=0.0249).
feature_014=1 response rate 0.173 vs feature_014=0 rate 0.166 (chi-square p=0.0315).
feature_030=1 response rate 0.183 vs feature_030=0 rate 0.168 (chi-square p=0.0643).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Probability of objective_response varies across categories of feature_005 (race/ethnicity), with at least one category differing from the rest.
Patients in feature_005='white' have a higher probability of objective_response than patients in feature_005='other'.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate response rates by feature_005: asian=0.163, black=0.157, hispanic=0.167, other=0.147, white=0.173 (chi-square p=0.0096).

analyses.code: two-proportion test for white vs other within feature_005
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=white response 0.173 vs feature_005=other 0.147; difference +0.026, contributing to overall chi-square p=0.0096.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Probability of objective_response varies across categories of feature_087 (insurance type), with at least one category differing from the rest.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_087: medicaid=0.169, medicare=0.172, private=0.167, uninsured=0.166 (chi-square p=0.540); differences are small and non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (a continuous covariate ranging 18-92, plausibly age) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
Patients with feature_085=1 have a different probability of objective_response than feature_085=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_078 68.03 in responders vs 67.98 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=0.717); no detectable association.
feature_085=1 response 0.168 vs feature_085=0 0.170 (chi-square p=0.505); no detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_018 and the probability of objective_response.
There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_096 and the probability of objective_response.
There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_064 and the probability of objective_response.
There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_045 and the probability of objective_response.
There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_042 and the probability of objective_response.
There is a monotonic association between the ordinal variable feature_125 and the probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho=+0.0040 between feature_018 and objective_response (p=0.372).
Spearman rho=+0.0026 between feature_096 and objective_response (p=0.556).
Spearman rho=+0.0043 between feature_064 and objective_response (p=0.337).
Spearman rho=-0.0072 between feature_045 and objective_response (p=0.109).
Spearman rho=-0.0040 between feature_042 and objective_response (p=0.369).
Spearman rho=-0.0104 between feature_125 and objective_response (p=0.020).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log1p(feature_063), feature_099, log1p(feature_092), and feature_035 are each independently associated with objective_response in a multivariable logistic regression; feature_078 is not.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit() with X = [feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log1p(feature_063), feature_099, log1p(feature_092), feature_078, feature_035]
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coefficients (response on positive scale): feature_057 beta=-0.383 (p=1.26e-96); feature_011 beta=-0.040 (p=1.82e-34); feature_006 beta=-0.006 (p=6.44e-20); feature_063 beta=-0.074 (p=7.26e-07); feature_099 beta=+0.118 (p=9.06e-07); feature_092 beta=-0.081 (p=2.16e-09); feature_078 beta=+0.000 (p=7.63e-01); feature_035 beta=+0.360 (p=1.66e-17). feature_078 is not independently associated; all others are.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the prognostic factors feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log1p(feature_063), feature_099, log1p(feature_092), feature_078, and feature_035, the race/ethnicity variable feature_005 remains jointly associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Likelihood-ratio test comparing logit with and without the four feature_005 dummies, controlling for top prognostic features.
analyses.result_summary: LR chi2(4)=13.21, p=0.010. The univariate race signal (p~0.01) is not detectable after adjusting for prognostic covariates (strongest single race coefficient: race_white beta=+0.077, p=0.140).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for prognostic factors, feature_087 (insurance type) remains jointly associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: Likelihood-ratio test for the four feature_087 dummies in the multivariable logit.
analyses.result_summary: LR chi2(3)=2.38, p=0.497. Insurance type is not associated with response after adjustment (univariate p was already non-significant, p=0.540).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (lack of) association between feature_085 and objective_response is consistent across categories of feature_005.

analyses.code: for each level of feature_005: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(feature_085, y))
analyses.result_summary: Within-race response by feature_085: white: 1=0.171, 0=0.175 (p=0.36); asian: 1=0.160, 0=0.168 (p=0.59); black: 1=0.160, 0=0.154 (p=0.59); hispanic: 1=0.169, 0=0.165 (p=0.67); other: 1=0.136, 0=0.160 (p=0.33). No within-race subgroup shows a significant feature_085 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favourable effect of feature_035=1 on objective_response is larger in patients with low feature_057 (i.e., the feature_035 x feature_057 interaction term is negative on the logit scale).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+0.0270 (p=0.674); the effect of feature_035 is approximately constant across feature_057 strata (no heterogeneity detected).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of feature_011 against objective_response is steeper (more negative) in patients with higher feature_057 (feature_011 x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=-0.0019 (p=0.710); no detectable heterogeneity in feature_011's effect across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs across feature_057 strata (feature_006 x feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=-0.0009 (p=0.330); no significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 and feature_006 interact: the joint effect on objective_response is non-additive on the logit scale (positive interaction would imply attenuation when both are high).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+0.000285 (p=0.093); a marginal positive trend (p~0.09) suggests possible attenuation when both are high but does not reach significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (small or absent) effect of feature_078 on objective_response varies with feature_057 (age x performance-status-like interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+0.0022 (p=0.189); no significant heterogeneity in the (already null) feature_078 effect across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_057 and objective_response differs in patients with feature_005='hispanic' vs the rest (race x performance-status interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term beta=+0.0023 (p=0.965); no detectable race-by-feature_057 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite linear-predictor score built from feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log1p(feature_063), feature_099, log1p(feature_092), feature_078, and feature_035 stratifies patients into tertiles whose response rates differ substantially: top tertile responds more often than bottom tertile.

analyses.code: Predicted-probability tertiles from the multivariable logit; two-proportion z-test top vs bottom
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by risk tertile (using positive-on-response sign): low=0.118, mid=0.165, high=0.224. Top vs bottom difference +0.107 (z=25.8, p=2.63e-147).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even after adjusting only for feature_057 (the strongest single prognostic factor), feature_005 (race/ethnicity) is no longer associated with objective_response, indicating the univariate race signal is largely confounded by feature_057.

analyses.code: Likelihood-ratio test for race dummies in a logit adjusting for feature_057 only.
analyses.result_summary: LR chi2(4)=13.97, p=0.007. Race effect attenuates to non-significance after adjustment for feature_057 alone (race_white beta=+0.074). Univariate race signal (p=0.0096) appears confounded by feature_057.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the ordinal variable feature_125 (range 0-4) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logit coefficient -0.0233 per unit feature_125 (p=0.038); a small but detectable monotonic decrease in response with increasing feature_125 (consistent with Spearman rho=-0.010, p=0.020).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across deciles of feature_006 there is a monotonic dose-response: response rate decreases approximately linearly from the lowest to highest decile.

analyses.result_summary: Decile response rates: D1=0.194, D2=0.181, D3=0.191, D4=0.178, D5=0.167, D6=0.164, D7=0.171, D8=0.147, D9=0.155, D10=0.143. Logit beta per decile = -0.0385 (p=2.55e-20); response falls from ~0.193 at decile 1 to ~0.143 at decile 10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single multivariable logistic regression including the eight prognostic factors plus feature_005 and feature_087 dummies, the prognostic factors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, log1p(feature_063), feature_099, log1p(feature_092), feature_035) remain independently associated with objective_response, while neither feature_005 nor feature_087 dummies reach significance.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit with prognostic features, race dummies, and insurance dummies
analyses.result_summary: Overall model LLR p=1.34e-173; pseudo-R^2=0.0189. Prognostic features remain significant: feature_057 beta=-0.383 (p=1.1e-96), feature_011 beta=-0.040 (p=2.4e-34), feature_006 beta=-0.006 (p=7.3e-20), feature_063 beta=-0.074 (p=7.2e-07), feature_099 beta=+0.118 (p=9.2e-07), feature_092 beta=-0.081 (p=2.4e-09), feature_035 beta=+0.362 (p=1.2e-17). Race and insurance dummy p-values: race_black=0.61, race_hispanic=0.49, race_other=0.26, race_white=0.14, ins_medicare=0.60, ins_private=0.57, ins_uninsured=0.86. After adjustment, no race or insurance category reaches p<0.05.
